title: Tapping for money shame
subtitle: A five-minute tapping sequence for the shame that arrives around money
excerpt: A five-minute tapping sequence for the shame that comes up around money. Debt, spending, earning less than expected, not having enough. The shame underneath the number.

practice.whenToUse: When opening a bank statement feels heavy, when money conversations bring a physical contraction, or when shame about your financial situation has been sitting quietly for a while

content.text: Money shame is one of the most private kinds of shame there is. Most people carry it without naming it. This sequence names it directly and works with the feeling rather than the number.
Setup
Tap the karate chop point and repeat three times:
Even though I feel ashamed of where I am with money, I accept that shame and myself.
Even though money has felt heavy and hard for a long time, I am willing to let some of that soften.
Even though my money story has been complicated, I accept myself and where I am right now.
Round one: name it
Tap through the points (eyebrow, side of eye, under eye, under nose, chin, collarbone, under arm, top of head) while saying:
This shame about money.
The way a number makes me feel like less.
The weight of not being where I thought I would be.
The silence I keep around this.
All of this shame that I've been carrying.
Round two: shift
My worth is not in my bank account.
Money is a circumstance. It is not a verdict.
Where I am with money does not tell the whole story of who I am.
I can work with this without hating myself through it.
I am allowed to feel okay even when the number is hard.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education.
